title: Proxmox Homelab Cluster
summary: The core of the homelab — a Proxmox-based hypervisor running all lab VMs, with VLAN-segmented networking and a structured VM inventory.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The homelab runs on a Proxmox hypervisor hosting all lab VMs across segmented VLANs. Built to mirror enterprise infrastructure for hands-on learning and experimentation.

sections.heading: Goals
sections.content: Provide an isolated, production-like environment for running security tools, Active Directory, and various self-hosted services without impacting the main home network.

title: Active Directory Lab
summary: Windows Server 2022 AD environment with domain controllers, GPOs, OUs, and scripted user provisioning.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: A full Active Directory environment built on Proxmox using Windows Server 2022. Designed to mirror enterprise AD setups for hands-on learning of domain administration, group policy, and identity management.

sections.heading: What's Included
sections.content: Domain controller setup, OU structure, Group Policy enforcement, DNS configuration, and PowerShell scripts for bulk user provisioning.

sections.heading: Goals
sections.content: Build familiarity with AD administration tasks commonly encountered in enterprise environments — user/group management, GPO design, DNS, and DHCP — as well as AD attack surfaces relevant to security work.

codeSnippets.label: Bulk user creation script
codeSnippets.code:
# Example: Create users from CSV
Import-Csv .\users.csv | ForEach-Object {
    New-ADUser -Name $_.Name `
               -GivenName $_.FirstName `
               -Surname $_.LastName `
               -SamAccountName $_.Username `
               -UserPrincipalName "$($_.Username)@lab.local" `
               -Path "OU=Users,DC=lab,DC=local" `
               -AccountPassword (ConvertTo-SecureString $_.Password -AsPlainText -Force) `
               -Enabled $true
}

title: Security Lab / Threat Hunting
summary: An isolated lab environment for practicing threat hunting, detection engineering, and incident response using Security Onion and simulated attack scenarios.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: A dedicated security lab running Security Onion for network security monitoring, IDS/IPS, and SIEM capabilities. Used to practice threat hunting against simulated attack scenarios in an isolated VLAN.

sections.heading: Lab Setup
sections.content: Security Onion deployed on Proxmox, configured to monitor traffic across the security lab VLAN. Attacker and victim VMs are isolated from the rest of the homelab network.

sections.heading: Planned Scenarios
sections.content: Mock threat hunting exercises including lateral movement detection, C2 traffic identification, credential dumping alerts, and log analysis workflows.

title: CTF Write-ups
summary: Capture the Flag challenge write-ups from TryHackMe and HackTheBox — methodology, tools used, and lessons learned.

sections.heading: About
sections.content: A running log of CTF challenges I've completed. Each write-up covers the machine or room, my methodology, tools used, key findings, and lessons learned. Focused on both offensive and defensive skill-building.

writeups.title: Blue
writeups.summary: Exploited the MS17-010 EternalBlue vulnerability on a Windows 7 machine. Good intro to Metasploit and understanding unpatched SMB vulnerabilities.

title: This Website
summary: The Astro site you're looking at — portfolio, project tracker, and hobby hub.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Built with Astro and deployed to Vercel. All content is stored as JSON files in the GitHub repo — no database, no backend. Covers projects, resume, books, board games, TTRPG campaigns, and learning progress.
